title: prunella vulgaris subsp. grandiflora
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. grandiflora, also known as large self-heal or big selfheal, is a subspecies of the self-heal plant (Prunella vulgaris) native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Prunella vulgaris subsp. grandiflora include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat various health conditions, with the plant typically harvested in the summer months when its leaves and flowers are in full bloom.
